title: clinopodium cimbricum
intro: Clinopodium cimbricum, also known as wild valerian, is a perennial herb native to Europe and parts of Asia, belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its antispasmodic, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Clinopodium cimbricum include relief from insomnia, anxiety, and muscle spasms, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and reduce menstrual cramps. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
